facts_disclosed: Cooks on charcoal stove — traditional method, better flavour

turns.content: okay so I need help na ka!! a food blogger want to interview me and they send me questions in English. one question is 'what is your secret to the perfect khao man gai?' and I want to give a good answer but sound natural not like a robot haha

That's exciting — getting featured by a food blogger is great exposure! What would you say is the thing that makes your khao man gai stand out from other vendors? Let's start with what YOU think the secret is and then I'll help you phrase it nicely in English.

honestly na ka the biggest thing is charcoal. I cook on charcoal stove. always charcoal. a lot of vendor now switch to gas because it's faster and easier to control, but I will never do that. charcoal give the chicken that smoky taste on the skin that you CANNOT get from gas

Oh that's a great answer — cooking over charcoal is becoming rarer among street food vendors so it really sets you apart. The smoky flavour it adds is something gas simply can't replicate. How about something like:

"My secret? I still cook on a charcoal stove. Most vendors have switched to gas, but charcoal gives the chicken skin a smoky flavour that you just can't get any other way. It's slower, it's harder, but the taste is worth it."

Want me to adjust the tone or add more detail?

ooh that is good!! but I want to add the part about my mom na ka. because this is not just my preference — my mom taught me charcoal is the ONLY way. she say if you cook khao man gai on gas you might as well sell something else haha. she is very strict about this

Your mom sounds like she doesn't mess around when it comes to the craft! That family angle makes the answer so much stronger. How about this version:

"I cook everything on a charcoal stove — always have, always will. My mother taught me that charcoal is the only real way to make khao man gai. The smoke gives the chicken skin a flavour you can't get from gas. It takes more time and effort, but my mom says if you shortcut the method, you shortcut the taste. She's right."

This keeps your mom's wisdom in there and shows it's a deliberate choice, not just habit.

YES this is perfect!! I love the 'shortcut the method, shortcut the taste' part. my mom would love that haha. you know what, I actually enjoy the charcoal process na ka. waking up and lighting the charcoal, waiting for it to be ready, the smell... it's like meditation before the crazy rush start. gas is just CLICK and fire. no soul

"No soul" — that's such a vivid way to put it. The ritual of it sounds almost meditative: lighting the charcoal in the quiet early morning before the chaos of the lunch rush. That's a beautiful detail.

Do you want to include that in your answer? Something about how the charcoal process is part of the rhythm of your day? Food bloggers love that kind of detail — the craft behind the food, not just the food itself.

yes add it!! but keep it short na ka because the blogger say max 100 words for each answer. also I spend like 200 baht per day on charcoal which is not cheap but I don't care. some cost you just accept because the result is better. my customers can TASTE the difference and that is what matter

Totally — some costs are non-negotiable because they directly affect quality. Here's a tighter version under 100 words:

"I cook on a charcoal stove — my mother taught me it's the only way. The smoke gives the chicken skin a flavour gas can't replicate. Shortcut the method, shortcut the taste. Every morning I light the charcoal before sunrise and wait for it to glow. It's my quiet moment before the rush. It costs more and takes longer, but my customers taste the difference. That's all that matters."

That's 68 words. Want me to change anything?

68 words is good, room for the blogger to add their own stuff too haha. I love it na ka. the other questions are easier — like 'how many years have you been selling' and 'what is your favourite menu item' — I can handle those. but the secret question is the important one

Glad we nailed the important one! And you're right, that's the question that'll make readers want to come find your cart. The charcoal detail is what turns a regular vendor profile into an interesting story. Readers will remember the image of you lighting charcoal before sunrise.

For the other questions, just answer naturally the way you'd tell a friend. Your personality comes through when you're relaxed. If you get stuck on any of the others, feel free to come back and I'll help polish those too. Good luck with the feature!

thank you na ka!! I am so excited for this. the blogger have like 50,000 followers so this is good for my business. I will send you the other questions if I get stuck haha. okay I go prep my charcoal now 🔥🔥 5am and the stove is calling me!!

Go light that charcoal! 50,000 followers is a solid audience — this could bring a lot of new customers your way. And you've given a great answer that shows real passion and tradition. That's what makes food content work — people want the story behind the plate.

Good luck with the interview and the morning rush! 🙏🔥
